what_user_likes.moving_background: Background is constantly moving like a lava lamp — organic, fluid motion that feels alive without being distracting
what_user_likes.varied_typography: Different typography and font sizes is refreshing — breaks the monotony of uniform sizing, creates visual rhythm and hierarchy through contrast

hero.structure: Full-width split-text hero with large headline, embedded autoplay video loop, and award badges (Awwwards, Clutch, CSSDA, DAN) along the bottom
hero.headline: We're a creative web design and branding agency based in London that crafts beautiful work for brands who refuse to blend in.
hero.video: Short preview loop on load (Short-Preview-homepage.mp4), full version on interaction, hosted on BunnyCDN

page_flow: Services (3 cards: Web Design, Branding, Digital Marketing)
Work/Projects (mixed grid: 1-col featured + 2-col smaller items with autoplay video cards)
Statistics (4 metric cards with animated rainbow gradient headings)
Partners (15 client logos with grid-item scroll animation)
layout_patterns.grid_system: Container-based with 12-column grid, container max-width: calc(1400px + 15%), wider variant: calc(1800px + 15%)

spacing.overall_feel: Extremely generous vertical rhythm — sections breathe with 150-170px gaps on desktop. Content never feels cramped.

primary_font.name: PP Neue Montreal (loaded as __montreal_a78db3)
primary_font.role: Body text, subheadings, navigation, general UI

display_font.name: Syne (loaded as __syne_ab8453)
display_font.role: Display headings, hero text, section titles — creates the dramatic size contrast

typography.pairing: Sans-serif + geometric sans display — both sans-serif but distinctly different character. Syne is wider, bolder, more geometric. Montreal is neutral, clean, readable.

letter_spacing.tight_display: calc(-30em/1000) — very tight for large display text
letter_spacing.loose_labels: calc(161em/1000) — very wide for small labels/tags

typography.weight_contrast: 400 for body, 600 for emphasis, 700 for headings — strong contrast between reading text and display text
typography.text_transform: uppercase used on services section heading for added drama

colour_usage.approach: Predominantly monochrome (black/white/grey) with strategic gradient accents — colour is earned, not default

background.blur_overlay: hsla(0, 0%, 100%, 0.43) with backdrop-filter: blur(30px) on desktop

accents.gradient_primary: linear-gradient(90deg, #633cc9, #3b8392, #cb24cc) — purple to teal to magenta, used for animated rainbow text
accents.gradient_secondary: linear-gradient(180deg, #ff34f3, #5d47fc 60%, #83e5e0) — magenta to purple to teal
accents.gradient_cta: linear-gradient(180deg, #6c3ce1, #ff34f3) — purple to magenta for blockquote accents

colour_usage.borders: rgba(0,0,0,0.3) on light, hsla(0, 0%, 100%, 0.3) on dark

scroll_animations.hero_stagger: Elements reveal with delay: 1.75s and 2s staggered entrance (Framer Motion)
scroll_animations.grid_items: data-animation='grid-item' triggers scroll-based reveal on project cards and partner logos
scroll_animations.mask_in_up: MaskInUp_container — text masks upward into view as you scroll
scroll_animations.parallax: Parallax_container — subtle depth on scroll for certain sections
scroll_animations.split_text: Words split and animate individually with translateY(calc(100% + 1rem)) start position

hover_states.project_cards: Video autoplay on hover/view (autoPlay, muted, loop)
hover_states.buttons: Color, background-color, opacity, border-color transitions at 0.2-0.3s
hover_states.tags: Border-color and background-color fill on hover, 2px solid border with 2rem border-radius
hover_states.cta_arrows: Arrow SVG shifts with translateX(-0.8rem) on hover

page_transitions.page_cover: #pageCover element with translateY(100vh) to translateY(0) — full-screen wipe transition between pages

interactions.overall_style: Refined and purposeful — animations serve hierarchy and delight without being gratuitous. Framer Motion for orchestrated reveals, CSS for micro-interactions.

premium_signals: Award badges prominently displayed: Awwwards, CSSDA, Clutch, DAN Network — establishes credibility immediately
Major client logos: Jamie Oliver, Penguin Books, Diageo, British Red Cross, Saatchi — social proof through association
13-year track record: 'Celebrating 13 years: 2013-2026' — longevity signals stability
High-production video assets hosted on BunnyCDN — not stock, custom for each project
Testimonial depth: full quotes with names, titles, company logos on coloured cards
Generous whitespace (10.5rem section gaps) — not cramming content, letting it breathe
Custom self-hosted fonts (PP Neue Montreal + Syne) rather than Google Fonts
Restrained colour: mostly monochrome with gradient accents as highlights, not decoration
Page transition wipe effect between routes — smooth, app-like navigation
Cyber Essentials certification in footer — signals security awareness
Quantified results section with specific percentages (67.6%, 70.8%, 83.14%, 104.9%)
Orchestrated Framer Motion animations with precise stagger timings — choreographed, not random
Max-width prose at 68ch — optimal reading width shows typographic care
backdrop-filter: blur(30px) overlay — adds frosted glass depth layer

fonts.primary: PP Neue Montreal (__montreal_a78db3), weights 400/600/700, self-hosted WOFF2

layers.name: WebGL background container
layers.description: Container for the animated background — likely a canvas element rendered by React client-side. CSS class found in stylesheets but element renders dynamically via JS.

keyframes.name: webgl-background_noise__uxurv
keyframes.description: Translates a noise texture across 10 random positions in 1.5s with stepped animation — creates organic grain/movement

layers.name: Spline 3D sphere
layers.description: A 3D animated sphere (likely Spline runtime embed) that creates the organic 'lava lamp' blob movement. Renders as a canvas element via Spline's React runtime. The sphere morphs and rotates continuously.

layers.name: Frosted glass blur overlay

css.base: position: absolute; top: 0; left: 0; width: 100%; height: 100%; background-color: #f8f8f8; z-index: 1;

layers.description: Semi-transparent white overlay with heavy blur — this is what makes the background sphere look like a diffused, organic lava lamp rather than a sharp 3D object. The blur softens everything behind it.

background_animation.how_lava_lamp_effect_works: The 'lava lamp' effect is achieved by layering a 3D Spline sphere (slowly morphing/rotating organic blob) BEHIND a frosted glass overlay (backdrop-filter: blur(30px) on semi-transparent white). The sphere's movement is visible but heavily diffused through the blur, creating soft, organic colour shifts that feel like a lava lamp. A noise texture is also animated over the top with stepped translate3d keyframes for added visual texture. On mobile, the blur is removed and a solid #f8f8f8 background is used instead (performance optimisation).
implementation_notes: Spline is a 3D design tool (spline.design) — the sphere is likely exported as a Spline scene and rendered via @splinetool/react-spline or @splinetool/runtime
The canvas renders client-side only (React component), which is why it doesn't appear in server-rendered HTML
The backdrop-filter: blur(30px) is the key ingredient that turns a 3D sphere into a 'lava lamp' feeling
Mobile gets a static fallback (#f8f8f8 solid) — blur is computationally expensive
The noise texture animation uses steps(2) for a deliberate low-frame 'film grain' aesthetic

typography_variation.approach: Dramatic size contrast as a design tool — headings range from 48px to 160px while body stays at 18-24px. This 7-10x ratio creates visual shock and hierarchy.
how_they_vary: Services section heading at clamp(3rem, 10.6vw, 10rem) — up to 160px, uppercase, tight line-height 0.9, tight letter-spacing — this is the most dramatic use
Hero h1 at clamp(2.5rem, 7.1vw, 7.5rem) — up to 120px but in sentence case, creating a different feel despite large size
Section headings at clamp(2.5rem, 4.26vw, 4.375rem) — a 'medium large' that provides rhythm between the dramatic and the readable
Body accent text at clamp(1.5rem, 1.8vw, 2.1875rem) — oversized body copy for impact statements
Standard body at 18px — clean and readable
Tags/labels at smaller sizes with very wide letter-spacing calc(161em/1000) — creates a completely different texture from the headings
contrast_techniques: Two distinctly different sans-serif fonts (PP Neue Montreal vs Syne) — same category but very different personality
Line-height varies from 0.9 (display, letters overlap vertically) to 1.4 (body, comfortable reading)
Letter-spacing swings from calc(-30em/1000) tight on display to calc(161em/1000) ultra-wide on labels
Text-transform: uppercase on services heading but sentence case on hero — same large size, different feel
Font-weight ranges from 400 (body) to 700 (display) — adds mass to the size contrast
Rainbow gradient text animation on key headings (statistics, testimonials, footer email) — colour draws extra attention
typography_variation.fluid_sizing: All heading sizes use clamp() with vw middle value — sizes scale fluidly with viewport, never jumping at breakpoints. This creates a smooth responsive experience.

structure.menu: Hamburger/overlay menu (hidden by default, data-modal-hide: true)

navigation.notes: Navigation is deliberately minimal — only logo and one CTA visible. Full menu is behind a hamburger. This keeps the focus on the content and the background effect. The long animation durations (1-1.2s) make the menu feel cinematic.
